result:
**Goal: Add a Coral Reef Ecosystem mode** — a multi-species marine ecosystem where coral polyps grow branching/massive structures, symbiotic zooxanthellae provide energy, herbivorous fish graze algae, predators patrol, and environmental stressors (ocean warming, acidification) trigger bleaching cascades and recovery dynamics.

This fills a clear ecological gap — the project has strong single-organism biology (morphogenesis, immune system) and simple predator-prey (Lotka-Volterra), but no rich multi-trophic ecosystem with habitat engineering. Coral reefs are the "rainforests of the sea" and produce stunning emergent spatial patterns: branching fractals, zonation by depth, algae turf wars, and dramatic bleaching/recovery cycles — all highly visual in a terminal grid.
